authors.author: England
England
Es wird nicht immer dunkel sein

properties.verseOrder: v1 v2 v3 v2 v4 v2 v5 v2

verse.name: v1
v2
verse.lines: O tidings of comfort and joy, comfort and joy, O tidings of comfort and joy.

verse.name: v3
verse.lines: From God our heav'nly Father, a blessed angel came; and unto certain shepherds brought tidings of the same: how that in Bethlehem was born the son of God by name,

verse.name: v4
verse.lines: "Fear not, then," said the angel, "let nothing you affright; this day is born a Savior of a pure virgin bright, to free all those who trust in him from Satan's pow'r and might."

verse.name: v5
verse.lines: The shepherds at those tidings rejoiced much in mind, and left their flocks a-feeding, in tempest, storm, and wind, and went to Bethlehem straightway, the Son of God to find.
